arms.predicted: adaptive_interpolation achieves worst-case calls = 7 (6 strategy + 1 confirm) for crossover scenarios and calls = 3 (2 strategy + 1 confirm) for the no-crossover scenario, with gap_throughput_rel = 0 for all 4 scenarios. Per-scenario: baseline=7, short-tight-ttft=3, long-loose-itl=7, small-queue=7.
arms.observed: baseline: M=40, calls=7, gap=0.0000; short-tight-ttft: M=256, calls=3, gap=0.0000; long-loose-itl: M=93, calls=7, gap=0.0000; small-queue: M=38, calls=7, gap=0.0000. Worst-case calls=7, no-crossover calls=3, all gaps=0. Exact match to prediction.

arms.predicted: ratio_binary_search requires exactly 9 calls (8 strategy + 1 confirm) for all 4 scenarios, confirming the baseline cost when neither interpolation nor early exit is present.
arms.observed: baseline: calls=9, gap=0; short-tight-ttft: calls=9, gap=0; long-loose-itl: calls=9, gap=0; small-queue: calls=9, gap=0. All scenarios at exactly 9 calls, gap=0. Exact match to prediction.

arms.predicted: adaptive_binary achieves worst-case calls = 9 for crossover scenarios and 3 for no-crossover. The 7→9 regression on crossover scenarios proves interpolation is load-bearing.
arms.observed: baseline: calls=9, gap=0; short-tight-ttft: calls=3, gap=0; long-loose-itl: calls=9, gap=0; small-queue: calls=9, gap=0. Crossover worst-case=9 (identical to ratio_binary_search), no-crossover=3. Exact match to prediction. Confirms interpolation accounts for the full 9→7 improvement.

arms.predicted: At m_max=512 (doubled range), adaptive_interpolation achieves worst-case calls = 8 (exactly +1 over m_max=256). Specifically: baseline=8, short-tight-ttft=3, long-loose-itl=8, small-queue=8.
arms.observed: baseline: M=40, calls=8, gap=0; short-tight-ttft: M=512, calls=3, gap=0; long-loose-itl: M=93, calls=8, gap=0; small-queue: M=38, calls=8, gap=0. Crossover worst-case=8 (+1 per range doubling), no-crossover=3 (unchanged). Exact match to prediction. Confirms sub-binary logarithmic scaling.

discrepancy_analysis: All four arms confirmed exactly. adaptive_interpolation achieves worst-case 7 calls (vs 9 for ratio_binary_search), a 22% reduction while maintaining gap=0. The ablation confirms interpolation is load-bearing: without it (adaptive_binary), call count reverts to 9 for crossover scenarios. The robustness test confirms +1 call per range doubling, matching the O(log log N) scaling prediction. The Pareto front remains at (3, 2.47%) predictor_naive and (5, ~0%) predictor_direct — adaptive_interpolation at (7, 0%) is dominated by predictor_direct but establishes the best-without-predictor position.
